signals.headline: **Prediction Market Impact**: These confirmed events (ceasefire extension + institutional BTC buy today) signal reduced
signals.raw: **Prediction Market Impact**: These confirmed events (ceasefire extension + institutional BTC buy today) signal reduced macro risk and fresh inflows, directly moving crypto prediction markets higher amid robust ETF data ($996M BTC, $276M ETH last week)[1][3][4].

signals.headline: **Key Catalysts Driving Rally**: Trump's indefinite extension of the Iran ceasefire removed the Hormuz Strait geopolitic
signals.raw: **Key Catalysts Driving Rally**: Trump's indefinite extension of the Iran ceasefire removed the Hormuz Strait geopolitical risk premium, boosting risk-on sentiment across crypto; Strategy's BTC purchase lifted holdings past 815,000 coins, absorbing miner supply and forcing shorts to unwind above $76K breakout[1].
matched_markets: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of April?

signals.headline: **Ethereum Specifics**: ETH at $2,390 (up 0
signals.raw: **Ethereum Specifics**: ETH at $2,390 (up 0.7% 24h), tracking BTC-led inflows but trapped in $1,800-$2,400 range; needs 200 MA break at $2,650 for bull case[1][6].

signals.headline: **XRP Specifics**: XRP at $1
signals.raw: **XRP Specifics**: XRP at $1.45 (up 1.5%, third rising session), testing $1.45 resistance wall of break-even sellers; prior gains tied to Rakuten listing (April 15) and SEC roundtable, with $119.6M ETF inflows week-to-April 11 and $65.89M in April[1][2][3].
